build._comment: Tauri loads the Expo Web build output. `npm run web:build` produces dist/ which is what frontendDist points at. For tauri:dev, devUrl points at `expo start --web` (default port 8081).

security._comment: CSP allows the prod + dev Workers (analytics, figure, vault, wantlist, devices), Clerk (sign-in), and Google Fonts (Inter / Bebas Neue). 'self' covers the bundled web build. The two `unsafe-inline` allowances are required for react-native-web's runtime style injection and Expo's font loader respectively.
security.csp: default-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' https://figurepinner-api.bubs960.workers.dev https://figurepinner-api-dev.bubs960.workers.dev https://*.clerk.accounts.dev https://*.clerk.com; img-src 'self' data: blob: https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval' https://*.clerk.accounts.dev https://*.clerk.com; frame-src https://*.clerk.accounts.dev https://*.clerk.com

bundle.shortDescription: Real eBay prices for action figures.
bundle.longDescription: FigurePinner shows real eBay sold prices for action figures. Search anything, see comps, no fluff.

windows._comment: WiX installer for Windows. Replace certificateThumbprint with the SHA-1 of an EV cert once one is purchased — without it the .msi installer ships with a SmartScreen warning until enough users accept it.

macOS._comment: minimumSystemVersion gates the .dmg to macOS 10.15+ (Catalina), which is what Tauri's webview2/wkwebview baseline supports. signingIdentity stays empty until the Apple Developer cert is wired — unsigned .dmg works via right-click → Open for users who accept the prompt.
